---
title: "SpaceX Locks Google Into A $920 Million-Per-Month Compute Deal After Anthropic, As xAI Abandons Colossus 1’s Messy GPU Mix - Wccftech"
date: 2026-06-05 23:51:00 +0000

description: "SpaceX and Google finalize a $920 million monthly compute deal, while xAI abandons its Colossus 1 project amid GPU challenges."
---

SpaceX has secured a significant $920 million monthly compute deal with Google, marking a pivotal moment in the AI and cloud computing landscape. This partnership comes on the heels of Google's collaboration with Anthropic, a company focused on AI safety and research. The timing is crucial as both companies aim to leverage advanced computing resources to enhance their AI capabilities and offerings.

The deal between SpaceX and Google is set to bolster Google's cloud computing services, providing SpaceX with the necessary infrastructure to support its ambitious projects, including satellite internet and AI-driven applications. This partnership underscores the growing demand for robust computational power in the AI sector, as companies increasingly rely on cloud services to scale their operations. Notably, the $920 million monthly figure highlights the financial stakes involved, positioning Google as a key player in the AI compute market.

In contrast, xAI, founded by Elon Musk, has decided to abandon its Colossus 1 project, which faced complications due to a "messy GPU mix." This decision reflects the challenges that many AI startups encounter when attempting to navigate the complex landscape of hardware and software integration. The abandonment of Colossus 1 may impact xAI's competitive standing, as it seeks to establish itself in a market already dominated by established players like Google and SpaceX.

The competitive context is further intensified by the ongoing race among tech giants to secure advanced AI capabilities. Companies like Microsoft and Amazon are also investing heavily in cloud computing and AI technologies, making the landscape increasingly crowded. As noted by [Wccftech](https://news.google.?oc=5&hl=en-US&gl=US&ceid=US%3Aen), the implications of these developments could reshape the competitive dynamics in the AI compute market.

Looking ahead, stakeholders will be keen to monitor how this partnership evolves and whether xAI can pivot successfully after its project setback.
